fact: August 13 is exactly 225 days into the year - that's like counting to 225 Mississippi's without stopping!
In the Northern Hemisphere, cicadas are typically screaming their loudest right now, making some of the most annoying sounds on Earth just to find a mate.
This date falls in the middle of Leo season, when the sun is in the Leo constellation like a cosmic lion flexing its muscles.
Sunflowers in Europe are often starting to droop their heavy heads because they're so stuffed with seeds - like nature's version of eating too much pizza.
August 13 hits peak spider season in many regions when they're getting fat and bold before winter.
In Australia, this date lands in the dead of winter when wombats are basically living like furry couch potatoes in their burrows.
The sun typically sets about 2 minutes earlier than it did just a week ago in most northern places - summer is sneaking away like a ninja.
Monarch butterflies often start their epic 3,000-mile journey south right around now, which is like flying from New York to London but with tiny wings.
August 13 sits exactly 140 days away from the winter solstice - you could count down like the world's longest advent calendar.
In northern regions, berries are usually bursting with so much sugar right now that bears are basically going on nature's ultimate candy binge.
The Perseid meteor shower often peaks right around this date, turning the night sky into nature's own fireworks show with shooting stars every few minutes.
Tree leaves in cooler climates are secretly starting to shut down their green factories around this time, even though they still look perfectly green to us humans.
